channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: NYC Department of Education builds the pipeline for future cybersecurity professionals
item.description: There’s been a lot of talk over the past few years about a shortage of skilled cybersecurity talent, but from where we sit at the NYC DOE Division of Information and Instructional Technology (DIIT), we see budding security pros all around us.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: Artificial intelligence’s recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should be using the technology and worried about whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk accuses media of encouraging people to assassinate him, Trump
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk on Sunday accused the media of encouraging the assassination of him and former President Trump with “relentless hit pieces.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Los CIO están bajo presión para ofrecer resultados de IA más rápidamente
item.description: Los directores de sistemas de información (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés) están sometidos a una presión cada vez mayor para generar valor empresarial a partir de la IA generativa, pero Juan Pérez, CIO de Salesforce, se encuentra entre los líderes de TI que creen que la impaciencia de los directivos
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Floating energy platform could change way you get power in future
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how NoviOcean's hybrid platform uses wind, solar and wave power to generate clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Buyer’s guide: Edge data centers
item.description: Edge data centers include hardware, software, applications, data management, connectivity, gateways, security, and advanced analytics. They come in all shapes and sizes depending on the use case: Small ones can fit into a wiring closet. Larger ones resemble traditional data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: An electric aircraft the military has its eyes on can take off with only 150 feet of runway
item.description: Natural disasters like Hurricane Helene in North Carolina illustrate the need for aircraft capable of accessing remote or disaster-stricken areas with minimal infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Phone bans in class wanted, but not all-day restrictions: Poll
item.description: 68% of U.S. adults said in a Pew Research Center survey conducted Sept. 30-Oct. 6, 2024 say they approve of such rules.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massachusetts judge rules Meta must face state's social media lawsuit
item.description: A Massachusetts Superior Court judge has ruled Meta must face a state lawsuit, alleging the company knowingly designed and deployed features harmful to young online users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Two Virginia congressional candidates debated an AI chatbot. It was shockingly rational
item.description: On Thursday night an AI chatbot participated in a political debate with candidates for a congressional seat, and, despite a few technical glitches, performed well. There was no robot voice, no off-topic tangents, no personal attacks, no odd “they’re eating the dogs” hallucinations.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok approved election misinformation ads despite its own ban, says watchdog
item.description: Just weeks before the U.S. presidential election, TikTok approved advertisements that contained election disinformation even though it has a ban on political ads, according to a report published Thursday by the nonprofit Global Witness.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: This gigayacht has a glass-bottom pool and stunning underwater lounge
item.description: An architecture firm has unveiled the concept for a 454-foot gigayacht that combines luxury, innovation and style and can accommodate 22 guests and 52 crew members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama man arrested for hacking Securities and Exchange Commission X account
item.description: Following the unauthorized announcement made during the hack, the DOJ said the price of bitcoin increased by more than $1,000 per bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, NewsNation

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Here’s how Cleary Gottlieb law firm uses genAI for pre-trial discovery and more
item.description: Corporate law is nothing like you see on television. To prepare for a case, 150 attorneys might be tasked to travel to remote warehouses to comb through tens of millions of documents gathering dust or track down amorphous electronic communications. It’s a process known as discovery.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Run a business? Then you need to join this Apple service
item.description: Apple just gave smaller enterprises a chance to compete on a more equal basis with larger concerns with three nice improvements to Apple Business Connect: Business Caller ID, brand identity in Mail, and custom business logos for use with Tap to Pay.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Feds seeks more guidance on post-quantum cryptography transition
item.description: Federal cyber experts say they need more formal guidance and a range of other tools for a post-quantum cryptography effort that's expected to cost billions. The post Feds seeks more guidance on post-quantum cryptography transition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: The Dark Side of Tech: Women in IT Struggle with Gender Bias and Discrimination
item.description: Women in the tech industry face significant challenges, including gender bias and discrimination, which hinder their career advancement. A recent survey by Acronis reveals the grim reality of the situation, highlighting the need for change.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to manage generative AI programs – governance, education, regulation
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence has grown extremely rapidly, attracting investment and sparking developer interest and creativity. Companies see generative AI as a route to building innovative services and revolutionizing their industries.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World
